title: Cashew, kale and lime nacho bowl
description: A vibrant nacho bowl topped with crispy tortillas, kale, sweetcorn, avocado and a zesty cashew-lime cream.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C. Line two large baking trays.
Cut the tortillas into triangles, spread over a baking tray, drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, black pepper and smoked paprika. Bake for 10–15 minutes until lightly browned and crisp.
Tear the kale into pieces, place on another baking tray, drizzle with a little olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Roast for 10–15 minutes until crisp.
Soak the cashews in boiling water for 10 minutes, then drain.
Blend the drained cashews with the juice of 1 lime, green chillies, coriander stalks, a good pinch of salt and 130ml water until smooth and creamy. Add more water if needed to loosen.
Cut the kernels from the corn cobs. Slice the avocado and squeeze over the juice of half a lime.
Scatter the kale over the baked tortillas, grate over the cheddar and place under a hot grill for a couple of minutes until the cheese bubbles.
Spoon over the cashew cream, scatter over the corn and avocado, and finish with coriander leaves and extra lime wedges.
